mission.title: cilium: CFP:  upload Helm charts to OCI registry and sign them with cosign
mission.description: CFP:  upload Helm charts to OCI registry and sign them with cosign. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current cilium deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cilium version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
helm list -n cilium 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cilium installation.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
## Cilium Feature Proposal

With Helm v3.8.0, the OCI support became [GA](https://helm.sh/docs/topics/registries), which is an excellent chance to start publishing Helm charts to OCI-compliant registries.

steps.title: Apply the fix for CFP:  upload Helm charts to OCI registry and sign them with…
steps.description:
Hi all,

Submitting this PR to have the Cilium Helm chart pushed to Quay.io as an OCI artifact.
The chart is then signed using Cosign (same process as for container images).

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CFP:  upload Helm charts to OCI registry and sign them with…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi all,

Submitting this PR to have the Cilium Helm chart pushed to Quay.io as an OCI artifact.
The chart is then signed using Cosign (same process as for container images).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
